'''Richard Russell''' (born 24 November 1967) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] and [[Castleford Tigers]], as a {{rlp|wg}}, or {{rlp|hk}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=209|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk |date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120227102926/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=209|archivedate=27 February 2012}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Wigan===
Russell made his professional debut for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 1985. He was used by Wigan as a [[utility player]].<ref name="Russell is hooked by hooking">{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Russell is hooked by hooking |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-russell-is-hooked-by-hooking-1408486.html |website=The Independent |access-date=8 May 2023 |date=22 January 1994}}</ref>

He played on the {{rlp|wg}} in Wigan's 8–2 victory over [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[1987 World Club Challenge]] at [[Central Park (Wigan)|Central Park]], [[Wigan]] on Wednesday 7 October 1987.<ref name="1987 World Club Challenge">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=452|title=1987 World Club Challenge|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012|archive-date=16 October 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071016210553/http://wigan.rlfans.com/fusion_pages/index.php?page_id=452|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Russell played on the {{rlp|wg}} in Wigan's 28–16 victory [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1987 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1987–88 Rugby Football League season|1987–88 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], on Sunday 11 October 1987.<ref name="1987-1988 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2647|title=1987–1988 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012|archive-date=17 April 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150417123124/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2647|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Oldham===
In July 1989, Russell was signed by his hometown club [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] for an undisclosed fee.<ref>{{cite news |title=Sport in brief |work=The Guardian |date=21 July 1989 |location=London |page=21 |id={{ProQuest|186918807}}}}</ref> He was initially signed by the club to play as a [[loose forward (rugby league)|loose forward]], but was also used as a [[hooker (rugby league)|hooker]], a role which would eventually become his preferred position.<ref name="Russell is hooked by hooking" />

He played as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]], (replacing {{rlp|FB}} [[Duncan Platt]]) in Oldham's 16–24 defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1989 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1989–90 Rugby Football League season|1989–90 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]] on Saturday 14 October 1989. He also played in the club's 30–29 win against [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[1989–90 Rugby League Divisional Premiership|1989–90 Divisional Premiership]] final.

===Castleford===
In August 1993, Russell was signed by [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in exchange for [[Shaun Irwin]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Young Lions lose Test and series |work=The Guardian |date=2 August 1993 |location=London |page=13 |id={{ProQuest|187418617}}}}</ref>

Russell played {{rlp|hk}} in Castleford' 33–2 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1993–94 League Cup (rugby league)|1993–94 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 22 January 1994.<ref name="Wigan 2 - 33 Castleford">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/viewreport.php?page=report&id=775|title=Wigan 2 – 33 Castleford|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

Russell left the club in 1999 to join [[Bramley R.L.F.C.|Bramley]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Lynx face Bramley in big kick-off |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6134256.rugby-league-lynx-face-bramley-big-kick-off/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=8 May 2023 |date=6 February 1999}}</ref>

===International honours===
Russell won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Castleford Tigers in 1995 against Wales.
